creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Stop Writing Tone Instructions. Layer Them. - Isadora Martin-Dye, Isadora & Co
videos.summary: Isadora Martin-Dye presents a four-layer architecture for maintaining consistent brand voice in AI systems: immutable identity, situational mode, example-anchored voice, and deterministic post-generation veto. The approach is grounded in production deployments across wedding venue, personal AI companion, and family-crisis applications.

videos.title: Turn 10,994 Notes Into Memory - Paul Iusztin, Decoding AI & Louis-François Bouchard, Towards AI
videos.summary: Paul Iusztin and Louis-François Bouchard present an AI Research OS, an open-source system that transforms an Obsidian vault of 10,000+ notes into actionable context for AI agents. The system uses a three-layer architecture (raw content, index, wiki) with deep research algorithms to index personal documents and web sources, then stores findings as memory that agents can query and maintain.

videos.title: Agents in Production: How OpenGov Built and Scaled OG Assist - Gabe De Mesa, OpenGov
videos.summary: OpenGov's Gabe De Mesa discusses building and scaling AI Agents in production across thousands of government organizations. The talk covers agent loop architecture, A2A protocol, Effect-TS implementation, evaluation frameworks, long-context handling, monitoring, tool building, and integration with Claude and Cursor for workflow acceleration.

videos.title: A Genius With Amnesia - Victor Savkin, Nx
videos.summary: Victor Savkin compares current coding agents to a genius with amnesia who can only see a tiny corner of a codebase and forgets between interactions. He argues that context limitations and memory loss severely restrict agent value regardless of underlying capability, and positions Nx and Polygraph as solutions to maximize agent autonomy.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
videos.summary: Cole Medin reviews Google's 50-page playbook on agentic engineering, arguing that the harness around an AI model determines 90% of results while the model itself accounts for only 10%. The video covers the spectrum from vibe coding to agentic engineering, the role shift from conductor to orchestrator, and token economics that justify harness investment.

videos.title: My AI Memory Now Follows Me Across Every Tool!
videos.summary: Walrus Memory is an external memory layer that persists context and memories across different AI agents and tools. It stores encrypted, user-owned memories that can be recalled by different AI providers without them knowing about each other, requiring only a single prompt to set up.

videos.title: The New Kimi K2.7 Model is VERY Impressive
videos.summary: Kimi K2.7 Code is an open-source coding-focused agentic model developed by Moonshot AI. Cole Medin reviews it as impressive for its price and size.

videos.title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
videos.summary: Advanced engineers use agentic loops where systems automatically prompt and iterate on AI agents without human intervention, scaling from single-agent loops to multi-agent orchestration patterns where orchestrators spawn parallel workers. This approach reduces manual prompting but increases token costs and requires robust observability and error handling.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: OpenClaw Creator's new secret project...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses OpenClaw Creator's new secret project involving Crabbox and a Codebase harness available on GitHub. The project is promoted through AI Builder Club and includes Superdesign, a web-based design tool.

videos.title: wtf is Loop Engineer & how to setup for real
videos.summary: AI Jason demonstrates Loop Engineer, a tool or framework for building AI agents with setup instructions. The video includes links to a GitHub repository with codebase and template, an AI builder workshop, and related resources.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: Video tutorial on constructing proactive AI agents capable of self-improvement through autonomous loops. Covers architectural patterns, memory systems, tools, and real-world use cases like autonomous ad optimization and SEO.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses emerging autonomous coding tools and features like the /goal command, Goal-buddy, and /Mission exploration capabilities. The video covers how these features work, setup, and prompting best practices for long-horizon coding tasks.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: It's about time we learn Transformers..
videos.summary: A video tutorial explaining how generative pretrained transformers (GPT) work under the hood. Covers fundamental components including self-attention, multi-head attention, feed forward networks, positional embeddings, layer normalization, and residual connections that power models like Gemini, Opus, and Qwen.

videos.title: Karpathy Bigram explained in 10min..
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code presents a 10-minute explanation of Andrej Karpathy's bigram language model, covering tokenization, embeddings, and training mechanics. The video introduces fundamental LLM concepts including loss functions, softmax, and the AdamW optimizer as foundational material for understanding GPT and attention mechanisms.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 in three sizes: Nano, Super, and Ultra. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer architecture, Multi-Token Prediction (MTP), and Latent Mixture of Experts (MoE) in their design.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released the M3 model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA), a shift from full attention to sparse attention mechanisms. The model includes tiled KV cache optimizations, continuous single-pass cache reads, and I/O improvements targeting reduced latency in both prefill and decode stages during inference.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
videos.summary: A system design interview framework that prioritizes problem understanding over technology selection. The approach covers seven sequential steps from requirements gathering through implementation constraints, using a URL shortener as a practical example.

videos.title: Your AI Agent Needs a Tool
videos.summary: An AWS video tutorial explaining how AI agents use tools to access real-time data and take actions. It covers tool calling mechanisms, schema design, system prompts, and the distinction between chatbots and agents that use tool calling.

videos.title: Agent Toolkit for AWS Explained in 2 Minutes
videos.summary: Agent Toolkit for AWS equips AI coding agents with tools, documentation, and guardrails to work with AWS services effectively. It provides up-to-date best practices and tested procedures to improve agent success rates.

videos.title: Running Claude Code on Amazon Bedrock
videos.summary: AWS Developers demonstrates how to run Claude Code, Anthropic's agentic coding tool, on Amazon Bedrock to keep data within AWS infrastructure. The walkthrough covers environment setup, cross-region inference, prompt caching, the Bedrock Mantle endpoint, cost tracking, and content governance via Bedrock Guardrails.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: A video discussing whether Google engineers use AI-assisted code generation, comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code, and speculating on Google's approach to code vibing across 2024 to 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses whether Malus, a company or tool, is ending open source development. The video interviews Mike about how Malus works and its impact on open source maintainers, framing the conversation around tensions between commercial interests and open source sustainability.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How Gong Built a $7B+ Company in the Age of Vibe Coding | Amit Bendov
videos.summary: Amit Bendov, CEO of Gong, argues that competitive advantage in software comes primarily from customer context, proprietary data, product experience, and operational complexity rather than code alone. He contends that fears of AI displacing SaaS companies are overstated and that the best AI products build passionate customer bases through meeting assistant capabilities and revenue intelligence.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a major advancement in robotics is approaching, but that the current focus on humanoid robots by companies like 1X and Unitree represents a limited vision. She contends that designing robots for human interaction and delight, rather than utility alone, will be more important as billions of robots enter society.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how a Paris-based AI performance management startup competes against enterprise software giants 600x larger by building a full platform from day one and leveraging trust through marquee customer references. She argues that even highly innovative companies like Google operate inefficiently on spreadsheets for strategic planning, creating the market opportunity for modern AI-driven platforms.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early customers do not indicate real product-market fit, and that genuine PMF manifests as customer pull (demand for pricing) rather than founder push. He advocates for embedded customer relationships over formal interviews and emphasizes betting on AI model trajectories rather than current capabilities.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: AI Could Delete EVERYTHING! Businesses Ignored This! #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof warns that agentic AI poses overlooked security threats including prompt injection, data deletion, and unauthorized actions. Businesses remain hesitant to adopt agentic AI despite its potential due to these risks.

videos.title: AI Podcast: Anthropic's Latest & Lisbon's Electric Vibe #shorts
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof's podcast episode "This Is Tech Year" discusses Anthropic's latest developments alongside guest Joao, with references to the Santos festival. The episode appears to cover recent news and trends in the AI company's work.

videos.title: Small Teams vs. Big Teams: Productivity & Meeting Madness! #shorts
videos.summary: Small teams practicing agile methodologies achieve higher productivity and task completion compared to large teams, which suffer from coordination overhead and excessive meetings. The video argues that efficiency is optimized in smaller organizational units.

videos.title: AI Memory Boom: Micron & SK Hynix Surge! Huge Growth Ahead! #shorts
videos.summary: A video discussing how AI demand is driving growth in high bandwidth memory, with Micron and SK Hynix positioned to benefit from this trend. The piece forecasts significant future growth in the AI memory sector.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
